title: NGE Super Robot Wars Alpha 3
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Super Robot Wars Alpha 3             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Mon Dec 21, 2009 5:02 pm Does anyone know if there are any american ports of SRW Alpha 3, Original Generations Gaiden(which I'm told includes Alpha 3), or MX? Are there any other recent SRW's that include NGE that I should be aware of?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Dec 21, 2009 9:43 pm The only thing ever getting officially translated and localized are the OG games. Regular SRW are a licensing nightmare outside Japan and noone will ever bother. As far as things go now only the first Alpha is somewhat fan translated, alpha 2 and 3 are jap only and will probably stay that way till the end of time. Ye olde SRW F also has Eva, with the hilarious stated height of 15m. EDIT: forgot that MX also has Eva, then the MX OST version of The Beast came around in my foobar shuffle.

[]:             Quote Post by GVman » Thu Dec 24, 2009 10:17 pm Super Robot Wars Alpha: The only thing ever to manage to fit EOE into its main plot twice .

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Thu Dec 24, 2009 10:34 pm There is a video on youtube where all the Gundam pilots attack 01 and lower her health to zero buuuuut then the heart beat starts and Eva 01 looks at the screen with her teeth out...So cool, she rips the Gundams apart and TI starts.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Thu Jan 07, 2010 12:44 pm As far as I know, Alpha 1 and F (for PS1) are the only games with NGE that are being translated, and no-one's planning to translate Alpha 3 or MX (which are PS2 games). However, MNeidengard has written some pretty detailed plot summaries that are available on Gamefaqs. The OG series only contains Banpresto's original characters, so even when it reaches Alpha 3's plot (which is bound to happen eventually), you won't get your Eva fix there. Eva's plot in Alpha 3 is apparently kinda crazy. I hear that conceptually it ties into the main plot kinda neatly, with Ideon's Ide being SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Starasp » Thu Jan 07, 2010 9:06 pm Anyone know if there any Emulators or something I could use to run this on my PC? I've been looking for this as well for some time.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sat Jan 09, 2010 6:16 pm Starasp wrote: Anyone know if there any Emulators or something I could use to run this on my PC? I've been looking for this as well for some time.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Jan 11, 2010 3:53 pm Swap Magic is too much of a hassle. Softmod the PS2 and play off the HDD. This has the additional benefit of no loading times. PS2 SRW games have a ton of stuff to seek on the disc and it can get quite annoying sometimes. No such problems with softmods

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Wed Jan 13, 2010 7:19 am PS2 emulation for Alpha 3 is good enough, since it's an old game and it doesn't move much. You might get some lag, but even a bad PC can run the game at normal speed. Rei is kinda bad, since she doesn't have Hot Blood/Valor, while Shinji is your leader for most of the game thanks to the AT Barrier bonus. Asuka becomes your boss killer because of her leader: attack boost, and she gains a post-movement ALL attack (though quite late) Unison Kick is one of your best attack, because of Shinji's Zeal/Awaken and Asuka's bonus. It only costs 120 will, too which is a bargain compared to most others. Much better than Alpha 1's incantation, obviously.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Mon Jan 25, 2010 11:44 am Swap Magic is fine for launch PS3 consoles, since you only need the disc to play backups, but that's a different topic. There's actually quite a few SRW projects in translation right now, only two of which (well, three actually) feature Evangelion. Aside from the already completed translations of SRW1, SRW3, and SRW Alpha Gaiden, SRW J is nearing completion, and SRW2, SRW4, SRW F/Final, and SRW Alpha are all in the middle of the process. And of those, F/Final and Alpha all have very relevant Evangelion plots. The only ones that have Evangelion and are excluded from that list are SRW Alpha 3 and SRW MX, and given the consoles that those are on, fan translations probably won't happen for another decade or so. Then there's also SRW Pinball, but c'mon. Who wants to play pinball on GBC with Eva units and Gundams?

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Fri Mar 05, 2010 7:01 pm Bumping this, because a friendly neighborhood tripfriend translator それも名無しだ over at /m/ translated some interesting bits and pieces from the Japanese SRW wiki. Firstly, the directly NGE-related parts (corporate fanfiction warning!): EDIT: Ugly-ass quotes replaced with a pastebin link.
